dedupe_key.symptom: cross-workspace read of ticket content and customer PII
dedupe_key.root_cause: TicketsController#show loads Ticket by id without scoping to the authenticated workspace

why_abusable_under_preconditions: show resolves the ticket via Ticket.find(params[:id]) and never authorizes it against the caller's workspace (no Pundit TicketPolicy#show gate, no current_workspace.tickets scope). Under the preconditions below, any authenticated member of one workspace can read another workspace's ticket content and customer PII by enumerating sequential ticket ids. Framed as a failing invariant: 'a ticket is only readable by its owning workspace' does not hold here. No exploit payload — the defect is the absent scope check.

preconditions.required_role/session: any authenticated NovuDesk user with a valid session
preconditions.tenant/account setup: two workspaces exist; the victim workspace owns a target ticket id distinct from the attacker's
preconditions.impact: read of another workspace's ticket content and customer PII (cross-workspace isolation break)

dedupe_key.symptom: a captured control-plane command can be replayed against nova-worker
dedupe_key.root_cause: DispatchTriageCommand signs the payload but does not include or verify a per-message nonce/timestamp, so signatures are valid indefinitely

why_abusable_under_preconditions: Commands are HMAC-signed over {ticket_id, action, args} but omit a nonce and timestamp, and nova-worker's verifier checks only the signature. The invariant 'each dispatched command executes at most once on the worker' is not enforced. Given the preconditions, an observed signed command (e.g. a benign re-route) can be re-sent and will re-execute. This is replay exposure, not an injection — no offensive tooling implied; remediation is binding a nonce+expiry into the signed payload and rejecting reuse on the nova-worker side.

preconditions.required_role/session: network position able to observe or capture a previously issued signed command (e.g. a compromised egress proxy or a logged transcript)
preconditions.tenant/account setup: a workspace with the Triage Gate dispatch channel to nova-worker enabled
preconditions.impact: re-execution of a prior triage/routing action on the worker

dedupe_key.symptom: agents backtrack to find the override-reason field, adding steps to every triage
dedupe_key.root_cause: override action opens the reason input below the fold with no scroll/focus, so the primary action appears disabled

evidence: screenshot: staging /triage suggestion review — override button enabled but reason field off-screen
measured: support-agent persona completed override in 9 steps vs the 5-step success_criteria; 2 backtracks and one mis-click on the disabled-looking submit across 3 runs
